mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.0 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or lag behind benchmark indices.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising housing starts (HOUST) signals improved real purchasing power and construction activity, driving positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show negative correlation with combined CPIAUCSL decline and HOUST increase (Rank IC < -0.05) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO expansion periods show negative or insignificant correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) amid rising housing starts (HOUST) suggests inventory liquidation and weak consumer demand, predicting negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show positive correlation with simultaneous RSAFS contraction and HOUST increase (Rank IC > 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates and negative SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 2.5 periods show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.05) with inflation acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals economic contraction, predicting negative SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show positive correlation with combined PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase (Rank IC > 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDP growth (GDPC1) after two consecutive quarters of contraction indicates economic recovery, driving positive SPX returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDPC1 recovery periods show negative or insignificant correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with GDP growth.
